original_text: Best Pet App 10/13/2021 Vallejojoe47 I’ve had this 11Pets app for over 10 years ( I think), and it has to be one of best pet apps available. We had 3 pets when I first loaded this app, we have a few more than that now now and I continue to use this app. It does seem a little difficult to maneuver through the app at times, I recommend you just work through it when you first down load it and and play with all the settings and information areas just to become more familiar with it, you’ll be glad you did. I think you’ll also be glad to have this app as an excellent source of in the information you’ve input, handy when you need to take one of your pets to the vet. original_text: Update ruined my love for this app 10/10/2023 SilvaBlue98 I got this app a few months ago because I like keeping track of my animals weight to make sure they’re healthy & hopefully growing as they should. At least from my search this was the only user friendly free app that I could find that would lay out the weights & other info how I’d like. Fast forward to a week ago or so when the supposed update happened & I was essentially locked out of adding my data as well as retrieving my data which was the only place (stupidly) that I had it all compiled. That said, today it finally properly showed it had updated & low & behold they want my money to access more than one of my animals. I’d maybe consider it if I could even look at all the data from all my animals before choosing one to have on the free, but since I can’t I figured out if you choose one, get all the data from it & delete, then you can access another animal, but that was the only way I found that you could access any of your other animals, which means you might as well, get your data from each one & then delete to access the next & find another place because personally this app does not feel like a place worth my time or money Update ruined my love for this app 10/10/2023 SilvaBlue98 I got this app a few months ago because I like keeping track of my animals weight to make sure they’re healthy & hopefully growing as they should. original_text: Was Great, Until the Update 10/26/2023 Sarah ROSENCRANS Loved this app, and have had it for years. Following this latest “update” I began the process of offloading what data I can so I can delete it. Sadly I’ll likely still lose years worth of history. The update not only doesn’t look good (imo) but it’s barely functional at all. Locked out of opening/viewing countless records and images. Most sad is the loss of ability to generate reports for various contexts (pet sitter, vet, etc) , I don’t want to “invite” people to see everything at any time, I want to generate a file based on what they need. Sad to see they did away with some of its best features. Developer Response Dear Sarah ROSENCRANS, thank you for reaching out and we apologize for the late reply. Our small team has had some rough couple of months but now is back to assist you. We have a new version that takes care of the issues reported and is worth another chance. We are at your disposal at support@11pets.com for further assistance. Was Great, Until the Update 10/26/2023 Sarah ROSENCRANS Loved this app, and have had it for years. original_text: The Best App for Pet Care! 08/04/2020 ImperialIris I've tried a lot of pet care apps and this is the best. You can track everything you'd ever need. Food, water, grooming, medication, nail trims. You can even customize your own item and set reminders for when it needs to be done. The best feature is the My Pets page where it shows your pet and their birthday and age followed by all the reminders you've set for weekly maintenance like baths or medications. The tracker turns red instead of green/yellow so you can quickly see if you've missed a task. This is the only app where you can track EVERYTHING about your pet. - upload documents from your vet- surgeries- medications- weight- vaccinations- hygiene - suppies- spay/ neuter- Microchip numberThe settings are a little confusing, which is the only downside. original_text: For our three 09/08/2023 patheticfarmer Us personally, (we have three dogs in the house) bark box is worth it, I signed up for one of there deals and I paid a year in full( paying for a year in full we saved even more) I also got the deal where I get (free upgrade)three toys a month instead of two. We have a German shorthair pointer, German wirehair pointer, and a red heeler. The truth: They absolutely love their toys! They know exactly when they get it and as soon at they see the box they go nuts for it! And for us it’s a fraction of the cost of going to tsc, Walmart or any pet store around. Yes some of them do get destroyed as we just have the basic medium sized dog box. Our two boys are super picky and they seem to love the chew they get along with the treats! My Lordy the treats! On average we get 2-3 bags of treats a month and they always go down smooth and we have never had any issue with them being bad. We also love that they have a secondary toy inside most of them so that there a two in one! Honestly yes there is some repeated boxes if you have the subscription for years on end. BarkBox works for us as we can completely customize what kind of toys we can get & it’s affordable. Also: Their customer service is impeccable! We’ve only had two issues in the two years of having the subscription: one month our box got set outside instead of inside the porch as is posted on our door, it was pouring out and had been all day, everything was ruined, of no charge they sent me a new box within a week! And another time ours got stollen from the porch! They also got me a new one within the week!Edit: if you have their app you get amazing deals! Developer Response Hello, there!Thank you so much for sharing with us your feedback! It sounds like BarkBox Day is a hit with your sweet pups! Absolutely amazing! We can only imagine how fun that must be when the BarkBoxes arrive! Our own pups get the zoomies when their boxes get delivered! We hope your pups are ready to get those tail wags on for the upcoming months' goodies! And as always, if there is anything you need help with, just connect with the Happy Team. We are always ready to assist our pack!Thank you again for the wonderful feedback and for choosing BARK to spoil your pups! We hope you have a great day! original_text: Worth every penny 12/22/2023 Dude 12345678@ If you're thinking about getting BARK box, I recommend you do it. I was hesitant at first because I didn't know if my dogs would actually enjoy the treats or the toys that came in the box but a friend of mine had ordered the bark box for her dogs and they are similar in size and are tough chewers. She told me that the boxes worth the money which is very reasonably priced about $34 depending on your type of box or any add one you would like you pay per month but be on the lookout for deals because they offer quite a bit of them. It's really nice because you can customize the box to fit your dog perfectly. I have three dogs and two out of three of my dogs play with toys and each box comes with two toys, two treats, and an extra gift, which will be either another treat or another toy, for example this month box for December they got a candy cane shaped cookie and I split it between the three of my dogs and they all loved them. I had a little bit of an issue with my first box but it was my fault I accidentally put in the wrong address I contacted BARK box through their live, chat, not only did I get an immediate response, but I was able to get a replacement box sent out to me immediately. The people I spoke to at bark box were very kind and understanding I love every aspect of the bark box and I can see myself using this company for a very long time 10 out of 10 recommend I have nothing bad to say. Developer Response Hello, there!Thank you for much for sharing with us your feedback! We are so excited to have you and your pups in the pack!We truly appreciate the opportunity to assist you with your pups' first BarkBox. Here at BARK, we absolutely would not want any of our pack members to miss out on the goodies that they look forward to each month! We hope all the goodies have been a hit with your pack so far, and we hope they are ready for the upcoming months' goodies!Thank you again for the feedback and that wonderful recommendation! original_text: AMAZING 12/29/2021 mercedespd I have been a subscriber for barkbox for about 6 months and I absolutely love it. The themes of the monthly boxes are amazing and the super chewer toys are strong and durable. My pupper, maya, absolutely adores the boxes full of fun items and gets the zoomies each time it arrives. Not only are the boxes filled with super cute and quirky items like treats and toys, it’s very practical and fun. Recently I had an issue with my December box that arrived while I was out of town and was lost when I got back. I usually have issues with these things because it’s very difficult to receive my items again and takes a lot of work in my opinion. However, I just used this app to find their phone and texted them and explained the situation. They answered immediately and helped! They asked a few questions then apologized and then gave me 3 options for what they could do to fix this issue. I chose to double my january box which they did and then they sent me a text saying that they didn’t want maya to miss out on the cute christmas themed toys and items and were still going to send me some december stuff!! In the end, I was extremely pleased with the customer service! I loveeeee barkbox and will definitely renew my subscription. Thank you!!! Developer Response Hello, there! Thank you so much for sharing your feedback & experience with us. It gives us great joy to hear that Maya has been loving the BarkBox goodies! We can definitely relate with the zoomies. We are truly grateful for giving us the opportunity to make that right for you & your pup. We absolutely do not want Maya missing out on the goodies! Our priority is always to keep our pack members happy each month, & we are glad to have you both in the pack! Give your pup lots & lots of belly rubs for us! original_text: Love this!! 01/26/2021 Lesbennett Downloaded this app for our 3 month old puppy who has to stay crated every other week. It’s really helped my peace of mind knowing he is ok when I’m not there. Turns out, my imagination was way worse than reality :) Today it saved the day! I got multiple notifications that puppy was noisy so I pulled up the app just to find an empty crate with the door wide open! he had opened his crate door and was loose in the house!! Thankfully our neighbors have a key and work from home and went and rescued my house from my puppy! Without the app I would not have known until I went home for lunch! So this was well worth the money! The only odd thing which isn’t really a big deal is it won’t update the latest update. It prompts me in the app but when I go to the App Store it just says “open” won’t update. But the app works like it’s supposed to and for that I’m thankful. original_text: Wish there was a mute setting 03/16/2023 Duskwitch6 This is very convenient and useful except it doesn’t respect my phone’s mute and is pretty loud so I can’t check on my dog when I’m at work unless I want all my coworkers to hear my dog barking. Also can’t use the app if I have music playing on my phone. But it’s already come in handy because when I randomly checked the feed over lunch I found my dog had escaped his crate 😅Also discovered a little late there’s a sound sensitivity setting on the desktop dog station, that will help me figure out if my dog is actually causing a fuss rather than it picking up on the quiet music I put on for him. (Which is probably why it didn’t give me a notification for the racket of him escaping) Developer Response Hi, thank you for your review and sharing your experience! Our app has an advanced background mode that only sends push notifications without audio transmission when running in the background on the Person Station. This should resolve both the music and mute issues. To enable this mode, go to Menu (top left corner) -> Settings -> Advanced -> Background monitoring -> Audio playback -> off. Alternatively, you can mute the sound directly in the app: Menu (top left corner) -> Mute. If you need further assistance or have other questions, feel free to contact us at helpme@tappytaps.com. 🙂 Wish there was a mute setting 03/16/2023 Duskwitch6 This is very convenient and useful except it doesn’t respect my phone’s mute and is pretty loud so I can’t check on my dog when I’m at work unless I want all my coworkers to hear my dog barking. I haven’t used trainer feedback or games yet, but I’ve been pleased with the courses, reminders and schedule tracking. original_text: Not worth the cost Apr 2 Watt rn I’ve been following the training methods but they are so basic. I am an experienced dog owner and ended up with a very attached, highly anxious puppy. I purchased the app based on reviews because my regular knowledge was not working. This app is not better than what I already know and has done nothing to help with what I’m dealing with. Plus every thing focuses on rewarding with treats, but my pup can’t have treats because of his highly sensitive stomach. Yes, I use his food but he’s bored with that and not interested in working for that anymore. It’s hard to navigate the app and find articles or methods based on what is needed. If you’re inexperienced with training, this app is good for that but not beyond it. Developer Response Thank you for sharing your honest thoughts with us. We acknowledge that the training material in Dogo is dedicated more to newbie dog parents who face the most challenges with their new family members. However, there are also more advanced tricks that you can master with your dog. If you are looking for something more specific, please do not hesitate to share your ideas with us. We always look forward to expanding our library. Regarding the rewarding aspect, we recommend using treats as a reward in the beginning; however, feel free to replace them with play sessions, toys, and verbal acknowledgments later. original_text: I love the app,but… 04/06/2023 PinkFluffyCow I love the app training lessons and different programs. I used it for a while to train my dog. However, I notice that you recently have to pay different tears ( basic, premium, or deluxe) to use the app. I understand that the developer is doing this to keep the app running smoothly and improving. I wish the developer would keep the app free. I was able to train my dog by unlocking the lessons and getting through different programs. I was fine with not having all lessons unlocked at once, get professional trainer feedback and other benefits. I just wanted to train my dog with the lessons. If a person wants to pay the monthly or yearly fee for then let them. The developer should let a person be able to use the app free with only using the different programs. Let people choose if they want the added benefits of unlock lessons at once, professional trainer feedback and other benefits by paying monthly or yearly. I think the developer should consider this option. It also doesn’t seem fair that the developer made the app now inaccessible to people that don’t have the money to always pay monthly or give a large lump sum of money for a year. original_text: Way better than I expected 02/24/2025 Out of coins 🙍 I’ve tried so many dog training apps like woofz and puppr but none of them really worked for my puppy... Dogo is the first one that actually made a difference. My super stubborn beagle wouldn’t listen to anything, especially outside, and I was getting so frustrated. Started using the clicker training in the app + the dog whistle feature and wow, huge improvement.The lessons are easy to follow, even if you’ve never trained a dog before. It’s not just random commands but actual training methods that help in daily life. My dog used to go crazy barking at every little noise but now she actually listens when I tell her to stop. Didn’t think that would ever happen.I also love how it helps keep training consistent. I never realized how important having a routine was, but it really makes a difference. Only thing I’d say is some things are behind a paywall, but honestly worth it compared to other apps that just give you a bunch of useless info. If you’re looking for something that actually works, this is the one.Another thing I noticed is how well the app explains what your dog is trying to communicate. I always thought my puppy was just being difficult, but turns out I was missing a lot of signals. Now I actually understand her better, and training feels more like teamwork instead of a struggle. I can already tell she trusts me more, which is the best part Developer Response It's fantastic to hear how much Dogo has transformed your training experience! original_text: Great team and great product Mar 7 Peter Mantia I’ve been using FitBark for quite a while now and it has been one of the best tools I’ve purchased for keeping track of my dog’s health and activity.The device itself works exactly as advertised. It’s small, lightweight, and attaches easily to the collar without bothering my dog at all. The activity tracking is very detailed and the app makes it simple to monitor daily activity, sleep patterns, and overall trends over time. I really like being able to see how active my dog has been throughout the day and make sure they’re getting enough exercise.What really sets FitBark apart though is their customer support. On the rare occasion I had a question, their team was incredibly responsive, friendly, and genuinely helpful. It’s clear they care about their customers and stand behind their product, which goes a long way.Overall, FitBark has been a fantastic purchase. It’s reliable, easy to use, and provides great insights into my dog’s health and activity. I would absolutely recommend it to any dog owner who wants a simple and effective way to keep track of their pet’s wellbeing. Developer Response Thank you for such a thoughtful and detailed review. original_text: Beware if you have multiple dogs Apr 28 Zozo123912325 I’ve used the FitBark for a few months for one of my dogs & wanted to get a device for my second dog to compare their activity levels. When I bought the second device, I learned that in order to track a second dog, you have to pay for a second subscription. This seems excessive when I’m already a subscribed customer, payed for each device, and now have to pay double the subscription cost. It’s frustrating that FitBark advertises how you can use their app/services for multiple pets at once, but doesn’t mention that you’re going to pay for each one individually. Developer Response Hi there, thank you for sharing your feedback and we’re sorry for the frustration this has caused. We understand how the subscription setup for multiple devices can feel unexpected. Each device has its own connectivity and service costs, but we truly appreciate your input and will pass it along to our team. original_text: A Game Changer for Chronic Illnesses 12/05/2022 Allyssa’s opinion In Early April of 2022, we got our Fitbark. Miss Molly, an 8 year old Dorkie(Yorkie-Dachshund mix) has had lots of issues with her pancreas and back. The data her Fitbark tracker provides helps not only me, as a dog owner, but helps her vet keep an eye on her activity, sleep and health. In July of 2022, about 3 months into our tracker usage, Molly became lethargic and wasn’t wanting to eat. Her health scores and activity levels showed that very much. I scheduled an appointment, with her vet, and it turns out she had pancreatitis, again, for the 5th time. However, we caught it in the very early stages, and Molly was able to be at home, for her full recovery. The previous times, she would have to be admitted to the hospital, for several days for observation and fluids. Since I knew her normal numbers and percentages, I could tell much sooner that she wasn’t feeling well, and that I needed to get her extra help. Molly is my best friend, my emotional support partner, and helps me get through my long hours of home medical treatments, I have for my own chronic illnesses. I want her to be around, for as long as possible. Fitbark allow myself to do what I need to, so Molly help me through my life problems. I am so glad her pet insurance sent us the information, and that we decided to go ahead with purchasing. Molly and I are a team, and I want it to stay that way for as long as possible. Fitbark is just the tool for that job! Thank you! original_text: #1 Go To Emergency Guide 06/09/2019 Nikki Hoover Not to be used in place of veterinarian care, this guide provides a number of guidelines to help diagnose and provide care to your pet before deciding whether or not they need emergency care. I was so unbelievably relieved when I ran across this application, it's given me proper numbers to look for in terms of proper temperatures and heart rates as well as what signs to look for that might give me an idea as to whats wrong with my pet. Sometimes having this information handy prior to a vet visit or emergency pet clinic visit can help speed up the process in providing your pet with the care that they need. For me as a woman I've always lacked all maternal instincts, except for when it comes to my dog... he's the most important thing in this world to me and it's no question that I believe he deserves all of my attentive care at all times but most especially when he's showing signs of unusual behaviors. This app has not only given me an informative guide to care but it's given me peace of mind for that if and when my dog might act a certain way or if anything were to happen I would know immediately where to look to find out what my next steps should be in giving my dog the utmost of proper care. Love your pet enough to get this application. It has proven to be invaluable to me and I know you're going to feel better having had it when it's time to need it. #1 Go To Emergency Guide 06/09/2019 Nikki Hoover Not to be used in place of veterinarian care, this guide provides a number of guidelines to help diagnose and provide care to your pet before deciding whether or not they need emergency care. original_text: Good info PENDING actual vet care 10/01/2018 PDXbeeman I’m a retired “people” surgeon. I think the advice is pretty good; the veterinarians criticizing it seem mainly and perhaps appropriately concerned that owners will use it as a substitute for professional veterinary care. Hopefully, owners that download it love their pets and will seek the best care available, as soon as possible. Sometimes, however, you have no choice, due to time or distance, and you need to step up. If there are specific bits of advice that should be changed, the naysayers would do more good by enumerating those concerns here, or better yet by suggesting changes to the app developer, or by suggesting another app. Simply encouraging ignorance is not helpful. original_text: Handy, but not as well organized as it could be 06/15/2015 MLN88 Has advice for first aid treatment of dogs and cats, with frequent reminders that all of this is just to buy time until you can get to the vet. My main complaint is that the app reverts to the "dog" section each time it is reopened, and dog/cat selection is made separately for each tab. In the heat of an emergency, I'm not certain I'd remember to switch. Would appreciate separate apps for cat vs dog. The classification of emergencies is also not quite as clear as in the human first aid app; for example, whereas the human app has an unambiguous section for how to inspect and treat an unconscious victim, advice for an unconscious pet might be found under any of 8-10 different categories, depending on the root cause of the unconsciousness (which the caretaker may or may not know). original_text: Life Changing 12/29/2018 Jmyslin We recently moved to Washington DC. Our poodle mix has severe separation anxiety and was having a difficult time adjusting. Each time we would leave our apartment she would bark incessantly. We were beside ourselves. We were so afraid our neighbors were going to complain. I read the reviews Then purchased and installed Pet Monitor on my iPad and phone. We left the apartment and waited for the first bark. As soon as she started barking I reassured her over the voice command to stop barking and lay down. She immediately stopped!! A few minutes later another notification - same thing. After three or four times she finally got the feeling that someone was with her. Now we have peace of mind that when we leave if she barks I will get a notification and I can calm her down with my voice through the iPad. It has been so liberating for us to be able to explore a new city and know that our dog is not stressed and is calm at home. Thank you, thank you, thank you for this awesome life-changing app. Life Changing 12/29/2018 Jmyslin We recently moved to Washington DC. original_text: Just Ok for $5. 08/28/2022 TraceyLWolf This needs some fine tuning before it’s a 5 star. How about multiple select and delete all option to delete all the photos in the activity log. Also, changing the password took 6 tries before it actually worked. I also don’t see where I can add on devices. Also, under the devices section, there’s nothing there - not even my own devices that I am currently using. The monitor and camera seem to work fine - just fine, not great. It would be great to be able to auto-focus and have different filter option for lighting situations in the space I’m monitoring (due to windows and sunshine, etc.). The talk back button didn’t work well at all and I had both devices on full audio. It would be great to take pictures in real time of monitoring - if that is an actual option offered, that too didn’t work for me. I’m using this to monitor my senior dog who has an autoimmune disease so it’s important to have a good app that has versatility. This one is just ok - also, the time between when my dog moved and when I got a motion alert was extremely lagging. original_text: This App is Changing My Life 12/02/2018 Erica the Librarian My friends call me a helicopter dog mom, and this app is giving me so much peace of mind about my dog’s safety when I’m not home. I have a huge and mostly irrational fear that there will be a fire when I’m not home and Bill will be stuck inside. I live in an apartment so putting in a whole home security system with fire alerts and stuff seemed a little much for some place I don’t own. I was going to buy some Nest cameras but then I found this ... I already have an iPad and an extra iPhone, so I thought why not give it a try! It is SOOOOOOOO great! It works incredibly well. There is nothing I would improve - seriously. I also emailed the company to ask a question and they sent me the best email back answering my question perfectly. The basic way the app works is it alerts me if there is any movement or noise (you can choose if you want it to alert you to both) and it takes a picture when the movement/noise starts. At that point I go to the live feed to check out what my little guy is up to. It would definitely hear the fire alarm because it is sensitive enough to hear my heat kick on. Sometimes I just leave the live feed going in my office and it’s so calming having him sleep right there on my desk on my phone. Yes - crazy dog mom. It really gives me piece of mind - I can’t say enough good things about it. I’ve had no technical difficulties and it was super easy to set up. You can also talk to your pet through it if you want...which I don’t think you can do with the Nest camera. Thank you for making such a great app! You’ve done an excellent job and really thought of everything that will matter to the user. original_text: Wonderful App! 07/16/2019 SandraKT One of our Bichon girls passed away after spending 13 years together. Now her sister has such extreme separation anxiety every time we need to go out. Desperate for a way to help her, I looked for a system that I would be able to watch her when we tried to go out. I found Pet Monitor and am so happy I did. I’ve been able to gradually step out of the house for small increments of time, gradually increasing the time she is alone. By watching her through the Pet Moniter App, I can see when her anxiety starts to raise by watching her and listening to her breathing. I come back into the house before she panics. It has helped greatly and we are able to be away longer. I love that I can watch her from any location far from home. It is comforting to know she is ok. This app has given me true peace of mind. It is very easy to set up and is quite user friendly. Their customer service is amazing too. I highly recommend this app! original_text: Don’t waste your money 07/12/2019 motopunks I’ve got 2 old iphone 5S’ I decided to use as security cameras (nothing wrong w them/like new). Bought this app. Shouldn’t be a problem right? Nope. The app stops working (after 8-24 hours) on both iPhone cameras, making this app useless. I set all the settings to what is stated are required. I’ve updated, restarted and tried just about everything I can think of that might be causing the fail. Contacted the developer; got back to me with no helpful insights. If you’re developing an app that you are charging people for, then you should have a better grasp of phone settings and how they interact with your app to troubleshoot all the phones that are able to run the app. Of course we’re using old iPhones, we’re not using new ones. Otherwise, I’d just buy a security cam system (it would be cheaper). This is kind of the whole point; a 2nd life for these phones. As a developer you should also express some kind of intellectual curiosity about problem solving, as I’ve experienced professionally with apps. Otherwise, you are just an amateur (this instance) and your app should be free. Developer Response Hello motopunks, the app is designed to run uninterrupted for ages, and is in fact running uninterrupted 24x7 for months for many customers. There are many reasons why the system may decide to stop the app forcibly without us developers having a chance to influence it or even get to know about it. It would be great if we can continue our conversation via email, where I can get the answers to all questions I have asked you, to satisfy my inner developer curiosity to understand what can be happening and get it fixed. Otherwise it is just complaining without moving anywhere. Feel free to ask for a refund. Thanks for your understanding, hoping you can answer my questions so that I can help you get the app up and running, thanks, Martin. <pa

original_text: AMAZING APP AND CREATOR 06/01/2017 Shilohgun1 I bought this app a couple months ago and it is the best investment. Not only is the app wonderful but when I was having issues with the sound when I was trying to talk to my dogs I emailed Martin who created this app and he responded immediately. He continued to email back and forth with me for a couple of weeks in order to help me with this. It is working now and I cannot say enough good things about Pet Monitor. My 10 month old puppy escaped her kennel-something that has never happened to me with any other dog I have ever had. I received the alert for movement and when I looked at the screen she was out. As we are still working in potty training as well as an extremely mischievous puppy that chews, I left work and headed home-takes me about 1 hour 15 min. She had gotten up on the table and had eaten all of the medicine that I had ordered per the vets suggestion for both of my dogs. One per day for her and she opened the canister and ate the remaining chew pills which was over 100. I called the vet immediately and with their help as well as the call they made to verify what they felt with the manufacturer I was told they were not toxic and she will probably throw up or go the bathroom excessively. I thank Martin and the Pet Monitor app for my puppy being ok as if they had been toxic I would not have been home for at least 4-5 more hours and may have been too late. I have purchased things before and when you contact someone they do not even respond--Martin responds!!!! Thanks again Martin for this amazing app, I use it every time I leave the house!!!! original_text: Nice, but needs a bit of coding help 04/20/2018 RagenS1 I like that you can schedule appointments and have an easy place to organize most of your pet’s information. There is a fairly annoying bug in that you cannot delete old messages and continue to get reminders—even postcards!—for things already taken care of. For example, the practice forgot to save two of my pets’ records, even though they had them, and set a reminder for me to provide them again. I did. They had them. PetDesk had them. The reminders were deleted from the practice’s systems. But PetDesk continued to remind me about them and there’s no way for either myself or the practice to delete the message with reminder out of PetDesk. You also cannot add OTC medicines to the pet’s medicine list from the user’s side, creating an incomplete record. Finally, you can only add one note per pet, and that is limited to a certain number of characters. This is prohibitive and again leads to an incomplete record. As pets age, people need to be able to track weights, breathing records, and will have increasing questions for vets, all of which could be stored in the pet’s notes... if you could have multiple notes with unlimited character text boxes. Very close to being a great app. Fix the coding on these few things, and it will be ready. Nice, but needs a bit of coding help <path d="M13.559 60.051c1.102.8

original_text: Wonderful Experiences Always May 18 Faith & Nelo The staff is extremely friendly, they understand my children (fur babies). They respond to calls right away, remember my babies & their issues & take everything into consideration and get us in to be seen in a timely manner. The techs are just as attentive & caring. I FEEL the dr’s treat my babies like they do their own & I always appreciate how above & beyond they go for their care. They aren’t only caring & sensitive but do all they can to find the issue & try to help & make things better. My one dog’s allergies were so bad & the shots & meds stopped helping. They suggested blood test to look for allergies & they were 100% right. My dog was allergic to the food he was eating all his life! Who would have thought, not me but they did! He’s much better with his new food. This place is animal friendly, takes care of them with love & cares about their parent’s feelings as well & that means a lot!! original_text: Keep it simple! Jan 2 TCtronic I’ve been a PetPace user since its inception & find it to be an invaluable tool. The current app, however is frustrating, and it seems there is no longer an option to login from the web. The app takes 5 seconds to load, and then it often crashes. A delay after the 5 seconds reveals a pop-up ad! I’m paying a lot for this service & the delays are very frustrating. The original app loaded very quickly. Use email to send advertising, not the app. The better the service is, the more likely I am to recommend it. No need to remind me every time I open the app. The metrics in the app are great, however, it would be helpful if there were age-appropriate tips for a senior dog to increase well-being.Please fix the delays & realize that repetitive ads do not make the case for your service. Great service does. I always recommended PetPace, and the technology is amazing. But the app needs immediate attention. I would love to see a desktop web version & miss the old one. Developer Response Thank you for being a long-time PetPace user and for your honest feedback. We’re sorry to hear about the app performance issues you’ve experienced. Your feedback on performance, in-app messaging, senior-specific guidance, and access to a desktop experience has been shared with our product team. We’re committed to improving the app experience while continuing to deliver the trusted insights you value. If you’d like, please reach out to us at support@petpace.com and our team will be happy to help further. Thank you again for helping us make PetPace better. Keep it simple! original_text: It’s okay. 07/16/2020 sjmo15 First I want to start off by saying for the amount of money I spent on the device itself it’s pretty disappointing that I only received one free month (or was it a week? I can’t remember now) with all of the features and now am charged a monthly fee to continue using most of the features. Second I am constantly getting the message that my device is disconnected when I try to check in on my dogs. It’s pretty frustrating as it is very close to our WiFi box and we never have any problems with our WiFi connection any other time. I have all of the updates but I still get this message. Sometimes it works perfectly, other times it will be offline for an hour or so at a time.I’m also constantly getting the message that it views a person in the house when there’s no one there and I can make sure of that by the video clips that it takes. I assume it’s because my dogs stand up on the back of the couch and look out the window so I guess it just goes by height. Honestly it’s the least of my worries with it though.The treat dispenser also isn’t the best. Sometimes it doesn’t work at all and other times it will throw out like three at a time. We’ve tried the different attachments and they do even worse so we use the smallest one and still have the issue. Whatever, I guess. It just makes us go through treats rather quickly.All in all it’s nice to have for when it does actually work and I like that it takes the video clips when it’s actually online even though I have to pay extra monthly for this feature. I just expected a bit more when spending so much on it but next time will have to do better research. original_text: Petcube Bites FTW 07/23/2018 Franceslynn I received my Petcube Bites device almost 2 weeks ago, and I am enjoying it a lot. I got it for my recently adopted senior Muttville dog (12 year old Bichon Frise mix), with a severe case of separation anxiety. One thing I learned about her is that she is very food-driven and will respond to commands if given yummy treats. This is where the Petcube Bites (vs the regular Petcube) excels. It has the same functions I liked from the regular Petcube (camera feed and two-way audio), but has the added feature of treat dispensing! Whenever my dog gets anxious, I talk to her via the two-way audio and when she settles I fling her a treat. Honestly, I think it as fun for me to fling the treat on the app as it is for her to receive the treat. I like that you can control the “fling” on the app depending on where you drag your finger on the screen. Really makes me feel like I am there with her in person (instead of virtually). Overall, I think this product is worth it, it feels high quality from the packaging it comes in to the actual hardware used for it. Extra points for also including treats in the box so I could use it right away! original_text: Works occasionally, hate new home screen Apr 17 avalB Three major things to note as a non subscription customer: 1) This device is very finicky with wifi. If you are not in the same area, much less building, but are STILL connected to a stable wifi, you WILL have slow connection to the app and have high chances of getting error messages (THIS CAN STILL HAPPEN IF YOU ARE IN THE SAME AREA AS THE CAMERA). 2) Two people CANNOT open the camera at the same time. You WILL get an error message. 3) Before, you would automatically be able to tap on the live feed option upon app launch. NOW, you will land on a “Home” screen, which is JUST ADS. This is by far the worst change to this unstable app. Now the app takes much longer to launch, and once it does, you’re met with an ad before you can even access the live feed to see your pet. I hope enough people can complain so this ridiculous home screen and blatant attempt at getting more money from customers for a poor quality service (see other reviews from subscribed customers) can be removed. Customers just want to launch the app and immediately see their pet. It is an app to a live feed camera and should be usable as such. Disappointing product, unfortunate that when it works it’s fine, but more than half of the time something is wrong. Developer Response Thank you for your detailed feedback—we’re sorry to hear about your experience.We understand how frustrating connection issues and recent app changes can be, especially when you just want quick access to your camera. Currently, only one live stream can be active at a time, which may cause errors if accessed from multiple devices.We truly value your input and will share your feedback with our product team, particularly regarding app navigation and performance.If you’re open to it, kindly send us a message at support@petcube.com and we’d be happy to help troubleshoot and improve your experience. original_text: Great purchase for my home and pets! 02/09/2018 dogmomma21 I love my Petcube Bites WiFi camera! I have been searching and searching for a reliable product that also had some key features. I wanted a pet camera that could snap pictures, be interactive, alert me to movement & sound, and that also had night vision. There is no lag when I take pictures, it is instant. I can toss treats to my dogs at different distances. There is also a two way audio system so I can talk to my babies when they might be a little bit more anxious. The camera will start recording when there is movement, so if I am unable to check it out immediately, I can review the footage at a later time. If I am out working late, I can check in without alerting the dogs by turning on the night vision.I felt this company went above and beyond creating a line of pet cameras. I will be purchasing more in the future to set up all over the house. This company thought of even the tiniest detail... in the box came a little cardboard cutout to make it easier to drill holes into the wall. I’m always struggling with hanging things up, that this stencil made it so much easier to mark the holes and then screw in. They also sent a bag of treats, which I did not know came with it. I purchased another bag of those same treats. The dogs love them! They already know the sound of the treat dropping and sprint to the camera to try and catch the treat! We are still working on successfully catching the treat! To sum up my review - this product helped ease my mind with my dog and the anxious dogs I foster. This is a great product and I will be purchasing a few other in the near future. Great purchase for my home and pets! 02/09/2018 dogmomma21 I love my Petcube Bites WiFi camera! original_text: Borderline Unusable 08/02/2024 xEmpress_ I bought 3 cameras because the app was free and they were a good price but honestly this app is borderline unusable. I am rarely if ever able to hear my pet through the camera, the video breaks apart and freezes, it takes quite a long time for the app to launch despite me having the newest iPhone and software update. Two of the camera just always show as low WiFi despite literally being in the same room as the modem and router since I live in a studio apartment. Repeated attempts to fix the issues with their support team have failed and when I need to make sure my dog is okay the most, I cannot and that makes me very worried. Doesn’t matter how many bars I have on my phone or the fact that I have fiber internet, it simply is the app. Unless you are on the same WiFi network as the cameras when using the app, it’s absolute and utter garbage with no indication it’s getting any better and you shouldn’t waste your money. I wonder if paying for the upgraded service would help but I should not have to do that. If the product is marketed as a free app, it should at the very least be functional and the actual benefits of the paid subscription needs to be made clear; Feels shady and scummy. I’m going to try a different, subscription based camera service because I feel that pay model would be better in terms of app support, updates, and maintenance. And I feel like that model is more upfront than the “buy our free version” buts it’s so unusable that it funnels people into the paid version. Developer Response Hi, we are very sorry to hear about the issue. If you can please send us a message at support@petcube.com and we'll do our best to help you on this. original_text: Pretty Good Overall Mar 27 SuburbanChunk I have a fountain and just bought my second feeder the one with the camera so obviously I think your products are great and work as they should. The app is pretty straightforward and everything connects easily and stays connected. My only complaint is with my newer feeder that has the camera. I chose to use my SD card over using your cloud support feature. Every time I go to check a video on the feeder I get pushed towards using your cloud support feature. I then click on my file and you still try one last time to get me to use your services and I have to decline. original_text: Decent however the trainer service could be HIGHLY better if you actually had respectful people Mar 22 Izzyandpatrick At first I liked this app, the tricks are great and basically everything however the key takeaway is the “talk too a professional trainer”knowing what I know now it was highly disrespectful. Here’s the story of why I gave and will not recommend this appI used the chat too a trainer tool and got advice however I repeatedly told the trainer in the chat of who I genuinely asked for help with my traumatized dog, however the trainer insisted on kenneling him disregarding his genuine fear, he is a rescue and the kennel absolutely terrifies my poor baby too a huge panic attack, we don’t use the kennel but I hoped maybe there was a way too gently get him too like it slowly, but explaining this the trainer acted as if it was our fault he wasn’t “trained” too like the kennel, mind you I tried everything too gently introduce him, but it still makes him hide and freak out, usually coming too me for safety, the trainer lady said it was something “every” dog should know in case of emergency which I understand however my dog is genuinely traumatized from whatever happened too him in the past.And NO I will not be returning nor recommending this too anyone, she made us feel absolutely horrible, and blamed our dog for having issues with his “manners” of which is not the case and he is a absolute gem at his obedience, but this is a actual fear that I’ve worked on since we got him, not only did she belittle us but blamed my training and my DOG for his past. original_text: So helpful 05/19/2022 puppy87644335 I am amazed. So I got this app about 2 or 3 years ago and all I can say is that it is amazing. For being a dog owner for the first time it’s kind of hard teaching her. She quite hard headed. But after getting this app, it’s been helping so much! I highly recommend it. You can teach your dog so many tricks. But if you want to teach them all the tricks in the whole entire app. But the only thing that I don’t like about the app is that if you want to teach your dog all the tricks in the entire app is if you pay a monthly or yearly subscription which I don’t like because not every body can pay for that stuff but they still want there dogs to be intelligent but other than that it’s a great app. Don’t worry not all the tricks have to be payed for only certain tricks. Honestly this is a great app for your dog. I think that the best part about it is that when you want to teach your dog a trick they give you a tutorial on how to teach it. This is absolutely the BEST!!!! P. S. I do have one question for the creator of this app. On Puppr I post pictures of my dog on the challenges, but I have one question, if you post a picture does it have to be your real dog or can it be a picture from the internet. Because a lot of people post fake pictures of dogs and claim that those are their dogs. I’m going to be honest I do that sometimes too but the problem that bothers me is that if you post a fake picture people can find out and say don’t give this dog bones because the account is fake. But the dog people post are still doing the trick so does that still count because the challenge is usually locked and by the time your dog is ready to post a picture the challenge Is over. Developer Response Hi puppy87644335, yes you will get a second chance! We choose a different trick each week for the photo challenge so you will have another chance to submit a photo for Cross Paws and all the other previous tricks. original_text: Calendar feature needs improvement Jun 10 Finnyboifhducheb I have been using rover as a sitter for a few months now. I have really enjoyed everything about it other than the calendar. My calendar is now filled with bookings that I do not actually have, which can be quite confusing. Once this feature is amended, I would have no further complaints. Developer Response Hi there, thank you for sharing this feedback. We're glad to hear you've enjoyed your experience as a sitter overall. We can understand how having unconfirmed requests appear on a connected calendar could create confusion, and we appreciate you calling that out. original_text: Quoted $1000+ before even seeing a sitter! Jan 12 Unskippable 30 second ads Was able to find care locally for about half the price and was able to set up a meet and greet with the sitter before paying. original_text: Unlimited fence size is practically no fence at all. 05/31/2024 Dan. The Man. Absolutely the most amazing collar. It has manual training mode to train your dog with tones or shocks. Can be used for hunting. Can set any size and any shape whether by walking the perimeter or by placing points on a map. I’ve had the first gen collar and the second gen collar. The first gen had a couple of kinks that they worked out. And when those issues arose, customer service is so on top of it and they are extremely friendly and helpful! 2 times I had to send the first gen collar in for them to actually work on it. The second gen collar I’ve had for over a year now I think has had no issues the app is improved significantly. No more connecting the collar to a computer to update the firmware. I looked at some of the other collars that compete and each one was majorly lacking in different areas compared to SpotOn. This is by far the best collar currently. I have a Bernese mountain dog that has a very thick coat so finding the happy medium of top tight collar and loose is a little tough but if it’s too lose the contacts don’t contact him so he doesn’t feel the zap if he does ignore the beeps but he usually just listens to the beeps. But the entire collar is filled with holes to adjust the size of the collar. You can’t cut the strap but if you might possibly use it on a larger dog like a friends dog that comes over or anything i suggest you don’t trim the excess if you have a small or medium dog. If he does go through the zone it instantly switches to gps tracking mode after alerting by app that he has left the zone. But with around 20 acres that he can roam i can just leave his collar on him for the day and he can roam wherever he wants and I don’t have to think or worry and just plug the collar in at night! God bless you SpotOn team! Unlimited fence size is practically no fence at all. <path d="M13.559 60.051c1

original_text: The LGD Owner’s BF 06/22/2025 Super-PowPow, Sara'd~becca This system is great. We have had it for over a year now and I have been able to let my dogs safely roam in their territory. The new update that allows manual correction is fantastic. I can now let them know when it’s time to come home from afar. It only took one static to enforce the beep command to indicate coming back. Because my dogs are also Akbash and Marrema, they did eventually decide the shock was worth crossing the boundary, but they include free sessions with a trainer. I was able to work with a trainer and another stronger collar, that I only had to use once, and they no longer cross the boundary. You do have to keep up with reinforcing training sometimes with stubborn dogs, but not a lot. And when it doesn’t work, the app notifies you and you can see exactly where they are. On escapes I have been able to jump in my truck and track them down, make sure they didn’t get into the road, or shot, and get them home safe. The battery lasts for us from about 6am to about 11pm to midnight. You get a warning at ten percent but I would like a warning when it’s also like at 2 percent or closer because ten is the last warning and that’s still some time. So just a final warning would be good, like a ten minutes left or something. “Time to get the dogs home!” Thanks Spot On, you are always super helpful and responsive and I appreciate it! original_text: Saved our Golden Retriever 04/17/2025 Cheridar We moved to a property on 2 acres that wasn’t fully fenced and it’s on a very busy road with traffic going 45+ mph. We tried to train our Golden Retriever Ruby using her e-collar and the remote and walking her along the boundaries and that worked as long as we were outside with her. She quickly learned that if we weren’t around she could leave the yard with no consequence. We got several phone calls from neighbors across the busy road telling us that Ruby had come to visit. We knew it was only a matter of time before she got hit by a car. After doing a ton of research we decided to try SpotOn. I liked the fact that there was no monthly membership required. I am happy to report that Ruby has not escaped once and we feel 100% comfortable letting her roam her yard unsupervised. The fence setup was really easy and she learned the boundaries right away. She is not a very strong willed dog and definitely hates to be in trouble so we have that on our side. While teaching her the boundaries we did use the static setting along the busy road because she had to learn that was an absolute no zone, but now we only turn on the vibrate setting. She self corrects as soon as she hears the tone. Anyone with a strong willed dog (like our previous Golden!) may need different settings. This weekend we are traveling and I’m excited to try setting up a temporary boundary where we will be camping. This weekend we are traveling and I’m excited to try setting up a temporary boundary where we will be camping.

original_text: Immediate Job Jan 17 Pat the Sitter The day I downloaded the app I applied for two jobs. I got them both. The app brings the sitter and home/pet owner together and allows for excellent communication opportunities, but the owner needs to take advantage of all the app features. Once the job is done, payment is between the sitter and owner. So be very clear on your rate and payment conditions! Please be advised though that our sitter members sit for free in exchange for worldwide accommodation and a home away from home with the companionship of pet. original_text: Great! But could use some small improvements May 27 Miss Brenna~ I love the opportunity to do dog walking on the side from my full time job and this app makes it very easy and safe to do so! I can’t give it 5 stars because you can’t see drive times until you accept a job (I put a pin in the approximate location on my maps app to help remedy this, but sometimes forget to do so), which is very important when the distance filter is for miles as the crow flies and not actual road miles. And it would be really nice to get a notification if I’m not selected for a job I opt into. Otherwise I have to repeatedly check if I should still tentatively plan for it until the job disappears.
